 {"id":"remote_compilation_helper-8qc.2","title":"CLI: Add config show --sources to Show Setting Origins","description":"## Problem
Users can't debug why a config setting has a particular value. With 5 precedence levels (default → user → project → env), it's impossible to know where a value came from.

## Solution
Add `--sources` flag to `rch config show` that displays:
```
$ rch config show --sources
enabled: true                    (default)
socket_path: /tmp/rch.sock      (default)
confidence_threshold: 0.75       (~/.config/rch/config.toml)
log_level: debug                 (RCH_LOG_LEVEL env var)
compression_level: 5             (.rch/config.toml)
```

## Implementation Details
Create ConfigValueSource enum:
```rust
enum ConfigValueSource {
    Default,
    UserConfig(PathBuf),
    ProjectConfig(PathBuf),
    EnvVar(String),
}

struct TrackedConfigValue<T> {
    value: T,
    source: ConfigValueSource,
}
```

Modify config loading to track sources as values are merged.

## Output Format
- Color coding: default (dim), file (normal), env (bright)
- Optionally show effective value vs all sources with --verbose
- JSON output includes source field

## Files to Modify
- rch/src/config.rs - add source tracking to merge logic
- rch/src/commands.rs - add --sources flag, update display
- rch-common/src/config/ - shared types for sources

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Every setting shows its source
- [ ] File paths are shown for file sources
- [ ] Env var names shown for env sources
- [ ] --json output includes sources
- [ ] Works with all config sections
","notes":"## Testing Requirements

```rust
#[test]
fn test_source_tracking_default() {
    let config = load_config_with_sources();
    assert_eq\!(config.socket_path.source, ConfigSource::Default);
}

#[test]
fn test_source_tracking_user_file() {
    // Create temp user config
    assert_eq\!(config.threshold.source, ConfigSource::UserConfig(path));
}

#[test]
fn test_source_tracking_env_var() {
    std::env::set_var("RCH_LOG_LEVEL", "debug");
    assert_eq\!(config.log_level.source, ConfigSource::EnvVar("RCH_LOG_LEVEL"));
}
```

### CLI Integration Test
```bash
# Verify --sources flag works
rch config show --sources | grep -q "(default)"
rch config show --sources --json | jq '.socket_path.source'
```

### Logging
Tests must log source detection for each setting.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:16:48.316674682-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T12:15:27.80096949-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-17T12:15:27.80096949-05:00","close_reason":"Completed","labels":["cli","config","ux"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"remote_compilation_helper-8qc.2","depends_on_id":"remote_compilation_helper-8qc","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:16:48.339678285-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"},{"issue_id":"remote_compilation_helper-8qc.2","depends_on_id":"remote_compilation_helper-f0t.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:19:33.115111168-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}
 {"id":"remote_compilation_helper-8qc.3","title":"CLI: Add rch diagnose Command for Classification Debugging","description":"## Problem
When a command isn't offloaded, users have no way to understand why. The hook is silent by design, but there's no diagnostic tool to explain classification decisions.

## Solution
Add `rch diagnose <command>` that shows:
```
$ rch diagnose "cargo build --release"

Command Analysis
  Input: cargo build --release
  Tool: Bash

Classification (Tier 4)
  Kind: rust_cargo_build
  Confidence: 0.95
  Threshold: 0.85 (from ~/.config/rch/config.toml)
  Decision: WOULD INTERCEPT ✓

Daemon Status
  Running: Yes (PID 12345)
  Socket: /tmp/rch.sock

Worker Selection (simulated)
  Available workers: 3
  Selected: css (speed: 1.2, slots: 24/32)
  Reason: highest speed score with available slots

Estimation
  Project size: 234 MB
  Transfer time: ~2.3s (at 100 MB/s)
  Expected speedup: 3.2x
```

## Implementation Details
- Parse command through same classification pipeline
- Query daemon for hypothetical worker selection
- Show each tier's decision
- Explain confidence calculation
- No actual execution - dry-run only

## Files to Modify
- rch/src/commands.rs - add diagnose subcommand
- rch/src/hook.rs - export classification functions for reuse
- rch-common/src/patterns.rs - add confidence explanation

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Shows classification decision with confidence
- [ ] Shows threshold and source
- [ ] Shows daemon connectivity
- [ ] Shows worker selection logic
- [ ] Explains why command would/wouldn't be offloaded
","notes":"## Testing Requirements

### Unit Tests
```rust
#[test]
fn test_diagnose_classifies_cargo_build() {
    let result = diagnose_command("cargo build --release");
    assert_eq!(result.classification.kind, "rust_cargo_build");
    assert!(result.classification.confidence > 0.9);
}

#[test]
fn test_diagnose_explains_threshold() {
    let result = diagnose_command("cargo check");
    assert!(result.explanation.contains("threshold"));
}

#[test]
fn test_diagnose_shows_worker_selection() {
    let result = diagnose_command("cargo build");
    assert!(result.worker_selection.is_some());
}
```

### E2E Tests
```bash
# With daemon running
rch diagnose "cargo build --release" | grep -q "WOULD INTERCEPT"

# Without daemon
rch diagnose "cargo build" | grep -q "Daemon: Not running"

# Non-compilation command
rch diagnose "ls -la" | grep -q "WOULD NOT INTERCEPT"
```

### Logging
- Log each classification tier decision
- Log confidence calculation
- Log worker selection reasoning","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:17:05.791150554-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T11:51:00.203875578-05:00","labels":["cli","debug","ux"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"remote_compilation_helper-8qc.3","depends_on_id":"remote_compilation_helper-8qc","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:17:05.822244072-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}
 {"id":"remote_compilation_helper-8qc.4","title":"CLI: Optional Hook Success Messages for Visibility","description":"## Problem
The hook is silent by design for transparency. But users can't tell if RCH is working - local and remote builds look identical. New users especially need confirmation that offloading is happening.

## Solution
Add optional visibility mode that shows brief success messages:
```
$ RCH_VERBOSE=1 cargo build
[RCH] Compiled on css (2.3s, 3.1x speedup)

$ cargo build  # Default: silent
```

## Configuration Options
```toml
# ~/.config/rch/config.toml
[output]
# none (default), summary, verbose
visibility = "summary"
```

Or environment variable: `RCH_VISIBILITY=summary`

## Message Formats
- `none`: No output (current default, maintains transparency)
- `summary`: One-line summary after completion
  - `[RCH] ✓ css (2.3s)` on success
  - `[RCH] ✗ local (daemon unavailable)` on fallback
- `verbose`: Detailed output during execution
  - Shows transfer progress, worker selection, timing

## Implementation
- Add visibility setting to config
- Check setting in hook output logic
- Write to stderr so stdout remains clean
- Respect --quiet flag (force none)

## Files to Modify
- rch/src/config.rs - add visibility setting
- rch/src/hook.rs - add conditional output
- rch-common/src/config/mod.rs - shared types

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Default remains silent (none)
- [ ] Summary mode shows one-line result
- [ ] Verbose mode shows progress
- [ ] Output goes to stderr
- [ ] Config and env var both work

-{"id":"remote_compilation_helper-8qc.5","title":"CLI: Better Circuit Breaker Status Display and Explanation","description":"## Problem
Users see "circuit: open" in worker status but don't understand:
- What does "open" mean?
- Why did it open?
- How long until it recovers?
- What can they do about it?

## Solution
Enhance circuit breaker display:
```
$ rch status --workers
Worker: gpu-1
  Status: degraded
  Circuit: OPEN (auto-recovery in 45s)
  Reason: 3 consecutive SSH timeouts
  History: ✗✗✗●○○ (last 6 attempts)
  Help: Wait for auto-recovery or run: rch workers reset gpu-1
```

## Implementation Details
- Track failure history per worker
- Calculate time until half-open state
- Show visual history of recent attempts
- Explain current state in plain language

## State Explanations
- `closed`: Worker is healthy, accepting jobs
- `half_open`: Testing if worker recovered (1 probe)
- `open`: Worker failing, waiting for timeout

## Files to Modify
- rch/src/status_display.rs - enhance circuit display
- rchd/src/health.rs - expose recovery timing
- rchd/src/api.rs - include failure history in status

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Circuit state has plain language explanation
- [ ] Recovery time countdown shown
- [ ] Failure history visualized
- [ ] Help text suggests actions
- [ ] JSON output includes all details
","notes":"## Testing Requirements

### Unit Tests
```rust
#[test]
fn test_circuit_display_closed() {
    let status = WorkerStatus { circuit: CircuitState::Closed, .. };
    let display = format_circuit_display(&status);
    assert!(display.contains("healthy"));
}

#[test]
fn test_circuit_display_open_with_recovery() {
    let status = WorkerStatus { 
        circuit: CircuitState::Open { 
            opened_at: Instant::now() - Duration::from_secs(30),
            timeout: Duration::from_secs(60),
        }
    };
    let display = format_circuit_display(&status);
    assert!(display.contains("OPEN"));
    assert!(display.contains("30s")); // recovery time
}

#[test]
fn test_failure_history_visualization() {
    let history = vec![false, false, false, true, true];
    let viz = visualize_history(&history);
    assert_eq!(viz, "✗✗✗✓✓");
}
```

### E2E Tests
```bash
# Trigger circuit open
# Check status shows explanation
rch status --workers | grep -q "auto-recovery"

 {"id":"remote_compilation_helper-8x0","title":"Unit Tests: rch/update/* - Update and Version Management","description":"## Overview
Unit tests for rch/update/* module - self-update and version management.

## Current Status: 21 tests exist, need 10+ more

## Files & Test Requirements:
| File | Current | Target | Notes |
|------|---------|--------|-------|
| check.rs | 3 | 6+ | Version comparison |
| download.rs | 1 | 4+ | Download with progress |
| install.rs | 3 | 6+ | Atomic replacement |
| verify.rs | 2 | 4+ | Checksum/signature |
| lock.rs | 4 | 6+ | Update locking |
| types.rs | 7 | 7 | ✅ Good |
| mod.rs | 1 | 2+ | Integration |

## Mocking Strategy
**Network calls**: Use mock HTTP server (wiremock) for GitHub API
**File system**: Use tempfile for binary replacement tests
**This follows the 'boundary mocking only' philosophy**

## Test Cases by File

### check.rs Tests (6 tests)
```rust
#[test]
fn test_version_check_newer_available() {
    init_test_logging();
    info\!("TEST START: test_version_check_newer_available");
    
    // Mock GitHub API response
    let mock_server = MockServer::start();
    mock_server.mock(|when, then| {
        when.path("/repos/owner/rch/releases/latest");
        then.status(200)
            .json_body(json\!({"tag_name": "v0.6.0"}));
    });
    info\!("MOCK: GitHub API at {}", mock_server.uri());
    
    let checker = VersionChecker::new(mock_server.uri());
    let current = Version::parse("0.5.0").unwrap();
    info\!("INPUT: current_version={}", current);
    
    let result = checker.check(&current).unwrap();
    info\!("RESULT: update_available={} latest={}", 
          result.update_available, result.latest_version);
    
    assert\!(result.update_available);
    assert_eq\!(result.latest_version, "0.6.0");
    info\!("TEST PASS: test_version_check_newer_available");
}
```

1. **test_version_check_newer_available** - Update found
2. **test_version_check_up_to_date** - No update needed
3. **test_version_check_prerelease** - Ignore prereleases
4. **test_version_check_network_failure** - Graceful degradation
5. **test_version_check_malformed_response** - Handle bad JSON
6. **test_version_check_rate_limited** - Handle 429 response

### download.rs Tests (4 tests)
1. **test_download_binary_success** - Full download works
2. **test_download_progress_callback** - Progress reported
3. **test_download_checksum_mismatch** - Reject bad checksum
4. **test_download_interrupted_resume** - Resume partial download

### install.rs Tests (6 tests)
```rust
#[test]
fn test_atomic_binary_replacement() {
    init_test_logging();
    info\!("TEST START: test_atomic_binary_replacement");
    
    let temp = tempfile::tempdir().unwrap();
    let binary_path = temp.path().join("rch");
    let backup_path = temp.path().join("rch.backup");
    
    // Create "current" binary
    std::fs::write(&binary_path, b"old_version").unwrap();
    info\!("SETUP: current_binary={:?}", binary_path);
    
    // New binary content
    let new_content = b"new_version";
    info\!("INPUT: new_binary_size={}", new_content.len());
    
    let installer = Installer::new(&binary_path);
    installer.install(new_content).unwrap();
    
    // Verify replacement
    let actual = std::fs::read(&binary_path).unwrap();
    info\!("VERIFY: new_content_matches={}", actual == new_content);
    assert_eq\!(actual, new_content);
    
    // Verify backup exists
    info\!("VERIFY: backup_exists={}", backup_path.exists());
    assert\!(backup_path.exists());
    
    info\!("TEST PASS: test_atomic_binary_replacement");
}
```

1. **test_atomic_binary_replacement** - Atomic swap
2. **test_backup_created** - Old binary backed up
3. **test_permission_preservation** - Executable bit kept
4. **test_rollback_on_failure** - Restore on error
5. **test_install_without_backup** - Fresh install
6. **test_install_readonly_directory** - Permission error

### verify.rs Tests (4 tests)
1. **test_checksum_valid** - SHA256 matches
2. **test_checksum_invalid** - SHA256 mismatch rejected
3. **test_signature_valid** - Ed25519 signature valid
4. **test_signature_invalid** - Bad signature rejected

### lock.rs Tests (6 tests)
1. **test_lock_acquisition** - Get update lock
2. **test_lock_contention** - Handle locked state
3. **test_lock_timeout** - Timeout on busy lock
4. **test_lock_release_on_drop** - Auto-release
5. **test_stale_lock_detection** - Detect orphaned lock
6. **test_lock_file_permissions** - Correct permissions

## Edge Cases
- Very large binary (>100MB)
- Slow network (timeout handling)
- Disk full during install
- Binary in use during replace
- macOS vs Linux permission differences

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All version comparison logic tested
- [ ] Download with progress callbacks tested
- [ ] Atomic replacement verified
- [ ] Rollback on failure verified
- [ ] Network failure handling tested
- [ ] All tests have structured logging","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T09:49:28.464621633-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T10:48:33.870777809-05:00"}

 {"id":"remote_compilation_helper-91n","title":"Benchmark Failure Recovery and Retry Logic","description":"## Overview
Implement robust error handling, automatic retry, and graceful degradation for benchmark failures to ensure SpeedScores are always available.

## Background and Justification
Benchmarks can fail for various reasons:
- SSH connection timeouts
- Worker resource exhaustion
- Disk full during benchmark
- Network benchmark partner unavailable
- Reference project compilation failure

Without proper recovery, workers could have stale or missing SpeedScores, leading to suboptimal selection decisions.

## Failure Scenarios and Handling

### 1. Transient SSH Failures
```rust
#[derive(Debug)]
pub struct BenchmarkRetryPolicy {
    /// Maximum retry attempts per phase
    pub max_retries: u32,
    /// Base delay between retries (exponential backoff)
    pub base_delay: Duration,
    /// Maximum delay between retries
    pub max_delay: Duration,
    /// Jitter factor (0.0-1.0) to add randomness
    pub jitter: f64,
}

impl Default for BenchmarkRetryPolicy {
    fn default() -> Self {
        Self {
            max_retries: 3,
            base_delay: Duration::from_secs(5),
            max_delay: Duration::from_secs(60),
            jitter: 0.2,
        }
    }
}

async fn run_benchmark_with_retry<F, T>(
    phase_name: &str,
    policy: &BenchmarkRetryPolicy,
    benchmark_fn: F,
) -> Result<T, BenchmarkError>
where
    F: Fn() -> Pin<Box<dyn Future<Output = Result<T, BenchmarkError>>>>,
{
    let mut attempt = 0;
    let mut last_error = None;
    
    while attempt < policy.max_retries {
        info!(
            phase = phase_name,
            attempt = attempt + 1,
            max_attempts = policy.max_retries,
            "Starting benchmark attempt"
        );
        
        match benchmark_fn().await {
            Ok(result) => {
                info!(
                    phase = phase_name,
                    attempt = attempt + 1,
                    "Benchmark phase succeeded"
                );
                return Ok(result);
            }
            Err(e) if e.is_retryable() => {
                warn!(
                    phase = phase_name,
                    attempt = attempt + 1,
                    error = %e,
                    "Benchmark phase failed (retryable)"
                );
                last_error = Some(e);
                
                let delay = calculate_backoff_delay(attempt, policy);
                info!(
                    phase = phase_name,
                    delay_secs = delay.as_secs_f64(),
                    "Waiting before retry"
                );
                tokio::time::sleep(delay).await;
                attempt += 1;
            }
            Err(e) => {
                error!(
                    phase = phase_name,
                    error = %e,
                    "Benchmark phase failed (non-retryable)"
                );
                return Err(e);
            }
        }
    }
    
    error!(
        phase = phase_name,
        attempts = policy.max_retries,
        "Benchmark phase exhausted all retries"
    );
    Err(last_error.unwrap())
}
```

### 2. Partial Benchmark Completion
When some phases complete but others fail:

```rust
#[derive(Debug, Clone)]
pub struct PartialBenchmarkResult {
    pub worker_id: WorkerId,
    pub completed_phases: Vec<(BenchmarkPhase, f64)>,
    pub failed_phase: BenchmarkPhase,
    pub error: String,
    pub timestamp: DateTime<Utc>,
}

impl PartialBenchmarkResult {
    /// Calculate SpeedScore using completed phases and neutral values for failed
    pub fn to_partial_speedscore(&self, weights: &SpeedScoreWeights) -> PartialSpeedScore {
        let mut scores = HashMap::new();
        let mut total_weight = 0.0;
        let mut weighted_sum = 0.0;
        
        for (phase, score) in &self.completed_phases {
            let weight = weights.get(phase);
            scores.insert(*phase, *score);
            weighted_sum += score * weight;
            total_weight += weight;
        }
        
        // Use neutral score (50) for failed and remaining phases
        let neutral = 50.0;
        for phase in BenchmarkPhase::all() {
            if !scores.contains_key(&phase) {
                let weight = weights.get(&phase);
                scores.insert(phase, neutral);
                weighted_sum += neutral * weight;
                total_weight += weight;
            }
        }
        
        PartialSpeedScore {
            total: weighted_sum / total_weight * 100.0,
            component_scores: scores,
            is_partial: true,
            failed_phase: Some(self.failed_phase),
            measured_at: self.timestamp,
        }
    }
}
```

### 3. Benchmark Error Classification
```rust
#[derive(Debug, thiserror::Error)]
pub enum BenchmarkError {
    #[error("SSH connection failed: {0}")]
    SshConnection(String),
    
    #[error("SSH timeout after {0}s")]
    SshTimeout(u64),
    
    #[error("Worker resource exhausted: {0}")]
    ResourceExhausted(String),
    
    #[error("Disk full on worker")]
    DiskFull,
    
    #[error("Compilation failed: {0}")]
    CompilationFailed(String),
    
    #[error("Network benchmark failed: {0}")]
    NetworkBenchmarkFailed(String),
    
    #[error("Invalid result: {0}")]
    InvalidResult(String),
    
    #[error("Worker unreachable")]
    WorkerUnreachable,
    
    #[error("Cancelled by user")]
    Cancelled,
}

impl BenchmarkError {
    pub fn is_retryable(&self) -> bool {
        match self {
            Self::SshConnection(_) => true,
            Self::SshTimeout(_) => true,
            Self::NetworkBenchmarkFailed(_) => true,
            Self::ResourceExhausted(_) => false,  // Likely to fail again
            Self::DiskFull => false,
            Self::CompilationFailed(_) => false,  // Deterministic
            Self::InvalidResult(_) => false,
            Self::WorkerUnreachable => true,
            Self::Cancelled => false,
        }
    }
    
    pub fn should_reschedule(&self) -> bool {
        // Should we try again later?
        match self {
            Self::ResourceExhausted(_) => true,  // Try when less busy
            Self::WorkerUnreachable => true,
            _ => false,
        }
    }
}
```

### 4. Graceful Degradation When SpeedScore Unavailable
```rust
// In worker selection algorithm
impl WorkerSelector {
    pub fn select_best_worker(
        &self,
        workers: &[WorkerState],
        config: &SelectionConfig,
    ) -> Option<WorkerId> {
        // Separate workers by SpeedScore availability
        let (with_score, without_score): (Vec<_>, Vec<_>) = workers
            .iter()
            .partition(|w| w.speedscore.is_some());
        
        if with_score.is_empty() {
            // No SpeedScores available - fall back to simple selection
            warn!(
                "No SpeedScores available, falling back to slot-based selection"
            );
            return self.select_by_available_slots(workers);
        }
        
        // If some workers have scores and others don't...
        if !without_score.is_empty() {
            info!(
                workers_with_score = with_score.len(),
                workers_without_score = without_score.len(),
                "Some workers missing SpeedScore, using available scores"
            );
            
            // Option 1: Only use workers with scores
            // Option 2: Assign neutral score to unknown workers
            // Option 3: Prioritize unknown workers for benchmarking
            
            // We use Option 2: neutral score (50)
            let neutral_score = 50.0;
            for worker in &without_score {
                debug!(
                    worker_id = %worker.id,
                    assigned_score = neutral_score,
                    "Assigned neutral SpeedScore to worker without benchmark"
                );
            }
        }
        
        // Normal selection with scores
        self.select_by_strategy(workers, config)
    }
}
```

### 5. Automatic Re-benchmarking on Failure
```rust
pub struct BenchmarkScheduler {
    // ... existing fields
    pub failed_benchmarks: HashMap<WorkerId, FailedBenchmarkInfo>,
    pub reschedule_policy: ReschedulePolicy,
}

#[derive(Debug, Clone)]
pub struct FailedBenchmarkInfo {
    pub worker_id: WorkerId,
    pub failed_at: DateTime<Utc>,
    pub error: BenchmarkError,
    pub attempt_count: u32,
    pub next_attempt: DateTime<Utc>,
}

#[derive(Debug, Clone)]
pub struct ReschedulePolicy {
    /// Delay before first reschedule attempt
    pub initial_delay: Duration,
    /// Maximum delay between reschedule attempts
    pub max_delay: Duration,
    /// Maximum number of reschedule attempts before giving up
    pub max_attempts: u32,
    /// Give up entirely after this duration
    pub give_up_after: Duration,
}

impl Default for ReschedulePolicy {
    fn default() -> Self {
        Self {
            initial_delay: Duration::from_secs(300),  // 5 minutes
            max_delay: Duration::from_secs(3600),     // 1 hour
            max_attempts: 5,
            give_up_after: Duration::from_secs(86400), // 24 hours
        }
    }
}

impl BenchmarkScheduler {
    pub fn handle_benchmark_failure(
        &mut self,
        worker_id: &WorkerId,
        error: BenchmarkError,
    ) {
        if !error.should_reschedule() {
            warn!(
                worker_id = %worker_id,
                error = %error,
                "Benchmark failed with non-reschedulable error"
            );
            return;
        }
        
        let existing = self.failed_benchmarks.get(worker_id);
        let attempt_count = existing.map(|f| f.attempt_count + 1).unwrap_or(1);
        
        if attempt_count > self.reschedule_policy.max_attempts {
            error!(
                worker_id = %worker_id,
                attempts = attempt_count,
                "Giving up on benchmark after max attempts"
            );
            // Notify user/alerting system
            self.notify_benchmark_abandoned(worker_id);
            return;
        }
        
        let delay = self.calculate_reschedule_delay(attempt_count);
        let next_attempt = Utc::now() + delay;
        
        info!(
            worker_id = %worker_id,
            attempt = attempt_count,
            next_attempt = %next_attempt,
            delay_secs = delay.as_secs(),
            "Rescheduling failed benchmark"
        );
        
        self.failed_benchmarks.insert(worker_id.clone(), FailedBenchmarkInfo {
            worker_id: worker_id.clone(),
            failed_at: Utc::now(),
            error,
            attempt_count,
            next_attempt,
        });
    }
}
```

## Unit Tests

```rust
#[cfg(test)]
mod tests {
    use super::*;
    use tracing_test::traced_test;
    
    #[traced_test]
    #[tokio::test]
    async fn test_retry_succeeds_on_third_attempt() {
        info!(test = "test_retry_succeeds_on_third_attempt", phase = "setup");
        
        let attempt_counter = Arc::new(AtomicU32::new(0));
        let counter_clone = attempt_counter.clone();
        
        let policy = BenchmarkRetryPolicy {
            max_retries: 3,
            base_delay: Duration::from_millis(10),  // Fast for tests
            ..Default::default()
        };
        
        let result = run_benchmark_with_retry("test_phase", &policy, || {
            let count = counter_clone.fetch_add(1, Ordering::SeqCst);
            Box::pin(async move {
                if count < 2 {
                    Err(BenchmarkError::SshTimeout(30))
                } else {
                    Ok(42.0)
                }
            })
        }).await;
        
        info!(
            test = "test_retry_succeeds_on_third_attempt",
            phase = "assert",
            attempts = attempt_counter.load(Ordering::SeqCst),
            result = ?result
        );
        
        assert!(result.is_ok());
        assert_eq!(attempt_counter.load(Ordering::SeqCst), 3);
    }
    
    #[traced_test]
    #[tokio::test]
    async fn test_non_retryable_error_fails_immediately() {
        let policy = BenchmarkRetryPolicy::default();
        
        let result = run_benchmark_with_retry::<_, f64>("test_phase", &policy, || {
            Box::pin(async move {
                Err(BenchmarkError::Cancelled)
            })
        }).await;
        
        assert!(result.is_err());
        // Should not have retried
    }
    
    #[traced_test]
    #[test]
    fn test_partial_speedscore_calculation() {
        info!(test = "test_partial_speedscore_calculation", phase = "setup");
        
        let partial = PartialBenchmarkResult {
            worker_id: WorkerId::new("test"),
            completed_phases: vec![
                (BenchmarkPhase::Cpu, 90.0),
                (BenchmarkPhase::Memory, 80.0),
            ],
            failed_phase: BenchmarkPhase::Disk,
            error: "Disk full".to_string(),
            timestamp: Utc::now(),
        };
        
        let weights = SpeedScoreWeights::default();
        let score = partial.to_partial_speedscore(&weights);
        
        info!(
            test = "test_partial_speedscore_calculation",
            phase = "assert",
            total = score.total,
            is_partial = score.is_partial,
            cpu_score = score.component_scores.get(&BenchmarkPhase::Cpu),
            disk_score = score.component_scores.get(&BenchmarkPhase::Disk)
        );
        
        assert!(score.is_partial);
        assert_eq!(score.component_scores[&BenchmarkPhase::Cpu], 90.0);
        assert_eq!(score.component_scores[&BenchmarkPhase::Disk], 50.0);  // Neutral
    }
    
    #[traced_test]
    #[test]
    fn test_graceful_degradation_without_scores() {
        let selector = WorkerSelector::new(SelectionStrategy::Balanced);
        
        let workers = vec![
            WorkerState { id: "w1".into(), speedscore: None, slots_available: 8 },
            WorkerState { id: "w2".into(), speedscore: None, slots_available: 4 },
        ];
        
        let selected = selector.select_best_worker(&workers, &SelectionConfig::default());
        
        // Should still select a worker based on available slots
        assert!(selected.is_some());
        assert_eq!(selected.unwrap().as_str(), "w1");  // More slots
    }
}

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Transient errors retried with exponential backoff
- [ ] Non-retryable errors fail immediately
- [ ] Partial results saved when benchmark fails mid-way
- [ ] Partial SpeedScore calculated with neutral values for missing phases
- [ ] Worker selection works when SpeedScores unavailable
- [ ] Failed benchmarks automatically rescheduled
- [ ] Alerting when benchmarks repeatedly fail
